summary: Good‑soundboard capture of a raw, adventurous show highlighted by a scorching Hey Pocky Way→IKO jam but marred by sloppy moments like Saint of Circumstance
review: The recording is a solid soundboard source with clear mix and no major audio flaws. Musically the concert feels charged and improvisational – the Hey Pocky Way→IKO sequence, Cold Rain and an enthusiastic Alabama Getaway stand out, while the second‑set Saint of Circumstance drags the vibe down and many listeners note overall sloppiness.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is uneven – some standout jams but several reviewers call the performance sloppy or one of the worst shows – while the recording itself is clean, yielding an overall rating around average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No significant audio complaints; mix is clear. One reviewer wondered about missing vocals after the first verse of Hey Pocky Way→IKO, but overall the sound is crisp and well‑balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: An oddball, adventurous set‑list that jumps from Foolish Heart into Alabama and the pocky/IKO sequence early; the show feels raw and inspired but often sloppy, giving a mixed sense of energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: One reviewer noted Jerry watching Brent’s zonked‑out keyboard solo with a puzzled look.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s organ/keyboard work on Hey Pocky Way→IKO is praised as “hot” and likened to ‘the Jerry Garcia of the keyboards.’
